routes.name: The Desert Crossing
routes.theme: Route 66 heritage and red rock country through the Southwest

highlights.name: Paso Robles Wine Country
highlights.description: Central Coast wine town with 200+ wineries and dog-friendly tasting patios

highlights.name: Tehachapi Loop Railroad
highlights.description: National Historic Landmark where freight trains spiral over themselves — viewable from Woodford-Tehachapi Road

highlights.name: Wind Farm Fields
highlights.description: Thousands of wind turbines stretching across the Tehachapi Pass mountain ridges

highlights.name: Roy's Cafe Amboy
highlights.description: Iconic Route 66 neon sign and gas station in the middle of the Mojave Desert

highlights.name: Route 66 Museum Kingman
highlights.description: Life-size dioramas and vintage autos celebrating the Mother Road's heyday

highlights.name: Hualapai Mountain Park
highlights.description: Pine-forested mountain park at elevation with dog-friendly hiking trails and cooler temps

highlights.name: Oak Creek Canyon Scenic Drive
highlights.description: 4,500-foot descent through Arizona's Little Grand Canyon with red rock walls and forest

highlights.name: Bell Rock Pathway
highlights.description: Easy dog-friendly trail with panoramic views of Sedona's most iconic red rock formations

highlights.name: Tlaquepaque Arts Village
highlights.description: Charming outdoor arts village modeled after a Mexican town — many shops welcome dogs

highlights.name: West Fork Oak Creek Trail
highlights.description: Shaded forest canyon hike with stream crossings — dogs welcome on leash, one of Sedona's best

highlights.name: Airport Mesa Overlook
highlights.description: 360-degree sunset viewpoint over Sedona's red rock formations and Verde Valley

highlights.name: Rancho Sedona RV Park
highlights.description: Creek-side camping at Rancho Sedona RV Park on the banks of Oak Creek. Wake up surrounded by red rock cathedrals, hike Bell Rock or West Fork Trail with the dog in the morning, wade in Oak Creek in the afternoon, and watch sunset paint the rocks from Airport Mesa. Wine tasting at dog-friendly Page Springs Cellars nearby.

highlights.name: Page Springs Cellars
highlights.description: Dog-friendly wine tasting with covered patio in the Verde Valley wine region

highlights.name: Standin' on the Corner Park Winslow
highlights.description: Eagles 'Take It Easy' tribute with Glenn Frey statue and red flatbed Ford — quick photo stop off I-40

highlights.name: Petrified Forest National Park
highlights.description: The most dog-friendly national park in the US — dogs allowed on ALL trails, plus BARK Ranger program

highlights.name: El Rancho Hotel Gallup
highlights.description: Historic Route 66 hotel where John Wayne, Humphrey Bogart, and Katherine Hepburn stayed

highlights.name: Albuquerque Old Town
highlights.description: Historic 1706 adobe plaza with galleries, restaurants, and the dog-friendly ABQ Trolley

highlights.name: Petroglyph National Monument
highlights.description: Thousands of ancient rock carvings on dog-friendly trails along volcanic escarpments

highlights.name: Fishers Peak State Park
highlights.description: Colorado's newest state park with 16+ miles of trails and 9,633-foot summit views

highlights.name: Spanish Peaks Vista
highlights.description: Twin volcanic peaks sacred to the Ute people, visible rising above the plains from I-25

highlights.name: Pikes Peak View
highlights.description: America's Mountain emerges on the horizon approaching Colorado Springs on I-25

routes.name: The Grand Canyon Route
routes.theme: Bucket-list national monuments through Arizona's canyon country

highlights.name: Paso Robles Wine Country
highlights.description: 200+ wineries with dog-friendly tasting patios along the rolling golden hills of Highway 46

highlights.name: Bearizona Wildlife Park
highlights.description: Drive-through wildlife park with bears, wolves, bison — dogs allowed in vehicles (windows up)

highlights.name: Route 66 Historic Downtown Williams
highlights.description: Last town on Route 66 bypassed by the interstate — six blocks of neon-lit diners and vintage motels

highlights.name: Mather Point
highlights.description: The most iconic Grand Canyon viewpoint — easily accessible with a dog on the paved Rim Trail

highlights.name: Rim Trail
highlights.description: 13 miles of paved, dog-friendly trail along the canyon rim connecting all major viewpoints

highlights.name: Desert View Watchtower
highlights.description: 70-foot stone tower at the canyon's eastern end with 360-degree panoramic views

highlights.name: Shoshone Point
highlights.description: Secluded 2-mile roundtrip hike through ponderosa pines to a quiet rim overlook — dogs welcome

highlights.name: Mather Campground
highlights.description: National park campground at Mather — the ultimate bucket-list camping experience. Watch sunrise paint the 277-mile canyon in gold from Mather Point, spend the day hiking 13 miles of dog-friendly Rim Trail, join a free ranger astronomy program under one of America's certified Dark Sky Parks. Book 6 months ahead on Recreation.gov.

highlights.name: Hopi Point Sunset
highlights.description: Arguably the best sunset spot on the South Rim — watch the canyon walls change color for an hour

highlights.name: Grand Canyon Dark Sky Park
highlights.description: Certified International Dark Sky Park — free ranger-led evening astronomy programs with telescopes

highlights.name: Cameron Trading Post
highlights.description: Historic Navajo trading post with authentic jewelry, rugs, pottery, and excellent Navajo tacos

highlights.name: Moenkopi Dinosaur Tracks
highlights.description: 200-million-year-old Jurassic dinosaur footprints you can walk on — free, with local Navajo guides

highlights.name: Monument Valley
highlights.description: Iconic sandstone buttes — 17-mile Valley Drive spectacular in late afternoon light, dogs allowed on leash

highlights.name: Four Corners Monument
highlights.description: Only place in the US where four states meet — Navajo vendor market with crafts and frybread

highlights.name: Mesa Verde National Park
highlights.description: 700-year-old Puebloan cliff dwellings — dogs on paved roads and campgrounds, not on trails

highlights.name: Durango Dog Park
highlights.description: 24 acres of off-leash paradise at Smelter Mountain with Animas River swimming access

highlights.name: Animas River Trail
highlights.description: 5-mile paved trail along the river through downtown — perfect morning dog walk with mountain views

highlights.name: Carver Brewing Company
highlights.description: Dog-friendly patio craft brewery — great burgers and house-brewed beer in historic downtown Durango

highlights.name: Smelter Mountain Trail
highlights.description: Moderate hike with expansive views of the Animas Valley — accessible right from the off-leash dog park

highlights.name: Million Dollar Highway
highlights.description: One of America's most spectacular drives — hairpin turns, 11,000ft passes, no guardrails, mine ruins

highlights.name: Silverton
highlights.description: Authentic silver mining town at 9,318ft with wooden boardwalks and saloons

highlights.name: Black Canyon of the Gunnison
highlights.description: Dramatic 2,000-foot sheer canyon walls — dogs allowed on rim trails and Cedar Point Nature Trail

routes.name: Utah's Mighty Five
routes.theme: Epic tour of Utah's national parks with Eastern Sierra gateway

highlights.name: Alabama Hills
highlights.description: BLM recreation area with iconic boulders against Mt. Whitney — dog paradise, all trails open

highlights.name: Mobius Arch
highlights.description: Short hike to a natural granite arch that perfectly frames 14,505ft Mt. Whitney in its opening

highlights.name: Mt. Whitney Portal Road
highlights.description: 13-mile climb to 8,374ft for close-up views of the tallest peak in the contiguous US

highlights.name: Bishop, CA
highlights.description: Eastern Sierra gateway town with Mountain Rambler Brewery (dog-friendly patio) and great food

highlights.name: Tonopah Stargazing Park
highlights.description: Named #1 stargazing destination in America — minimal light pollution, extraordinary night skies

highlights.name: Mizpah Hotel
highlights.description: Beautifully restored 1907 hotel — voted most haunted hotel in America, worth a stay or a look

highlights.name: Tonopah Brewing Company
highlights.description: 26 craft beers brewed on-site in this remote Nevada mining town

highlights.name: Zion National Park
highlights.description: 2,000-foot canyon walls — dogs allowed on Pa'rus Trail (3.5mi paved along the Virgin River)

highlights.name: Pa'rus Trail
highlights.description: Zion's dog-friendly trail — 3.5mi paved walk through the valley floor with towering red/white cliffs

highlights.name: Bryce Canyon National Park
highlights.description: Otherworldly hoodoo formations — dogs on paved Shared Use Path between Sunrise and Sunset Points

highlights.name: Scenic Byway 12
highlights.description: One of America's 10 most scenic drives — Red Canyon, the Hogsback ridge, and Boulder Mountain

highlights.name: Red Canyon
highlights.description: Red rock arches spanning over the road — a stunning prelude to Bryce Canyon

highlights.name: Capitol Reef National Park
highlights.description: Drive the 8-mile scenic road through Waterpocket Fold geology — pick fruit in historic orchards

highlights.name: Goblin Valley State Park
highlights.description: Surreal mushroom-shaped rock formations — dogs on leash, feels like walking on Mars

highlights.name: Dead Horse Point State Park
highlights.description: 8 miles of dog-friendly rim trails with 2,000-foot views above the Colorado River

highlights.name: Corona Arch Trail
highlights.description: 3-mile BLM trail to a massive natural arch — fully dog-friendly, most popular arch hike outside Arches NP

highlights.name: Arches National Park
highlights.description: No timed entry in 2026 — scenic drive past Park Avenue, Balanced Rock, and Delicate Arch viewpoint

highlights.name: Canyonlands Island in the Sky
highlights.description: Stunning canyon overlooks from a 6,000ft mesa — Grand View Point is one of the great American views

highlights.name: Grandstaff Trail
highlights.description: Creek-side canyon trail to Morning Glory Natural Bridge — dog-friendly with stream crossings for swimming

highlights.name: Moab KOA
highlights.description: Desert canyon camping at Moab KOA or Dead Horse Point — surrounded by red rock on BLM land with endless dog-friendly trail options. Morning on Grandstaff Trail (creek crossings, your dog will love the water), afternoon drive through Arches NP scenic road, evening watching sunset turn the La Sal Mountains pink from your campsite.

highlights.name: Glenwood Canyon
highlights.description: 12.5 miles of I-70 carved through 2,000-foot canyon walls — one of America's engineering marvels

highlights.name: Vail Village
highlights.description: European-style pedestrian village at 8,150ft — dog-friendly shops, Betty Ford Alpine Gardens

highlights.name: Eisenhower Tunnel
highlights.description: Highest point on the US Interstate system at 11,158ft — mountain views in every direction

routes.name: The Southern Sun
routes.theme: Saguaro cactus forests, white gypsum dunes, and mountain pine forest camping through the deep South

highlights.name: Hearst Castle Vista Point
highlights.description: Scenic pullout with views of the Hearst Castle estate above and elephant seals on the beach below — dogs welcome at the vista

highlights.name: Morro Rock & Morro Bay
highlights.description: Iconic 576-foot volcanic plug — dog-friendly Morro Strand State Beach and waterfront boardwalk

highlights.name: Santa Barbara Waterfront
highlights.description: East Beach and Stearns Wharf — dogs welcome on the beach before 10am and after 4pm, walk the historic pier

highlights.name: Route 66 Dog Park Kingman
highlights.description: Themed off-leash dog park with miniature Route 66 drive-through and water features — let Sabby run

highlights.name: Arizona Route 66 Museum
highlights.description: Life-size dioramas in the 1907 Powerhouse building — the definitive Route 66 museum

highlights.name: Saguaro National Park West
highlights.description: 40-foot saguaro cactus forests found nowhere else on Earth — dogs on paved Desert Discovery Trail

highlights.name: Mount Lemmon Scenic Drive
highlights.description: Drive from desert floor to 9,157ft pine forest — a 30-degree temperature drop in 30 miles

highlights.name: Catalina State Park
highlights.description: 8 dog-friendly trails through Sonoran desert landscape under the Santa Catalina Mountains

highlights.name: White Sands National Park
highlights.description: 275 sq miles of white gypsum dunes — dogs allowed on ALL trails and dunes, one of the most dog-friendly NPS sites

highlights.name: US-82 Cloudcroft Highway
highlights.description: 19-mile climb from Chihuahuan desert floor (4,350ft) to pine forest (8,600ft) — dramatic transition

highlights.name: Osha Trail
highlights.description: 2.5-mile mountain loop with views looking down at White Sands dunes far below — dogs welcome

highlights.name: Dog Canyon Trail
highlights.description: Forested canyon trail in Lincoln National Forest — aptly named, dogs love the shaded cool hike

highlights.name: Rim Trail Cloudcroft
highlights.description: 13.5-mile Sacramento Mountain rim trail through ponderosa pines — hike sections with the dog

highlights.name: 16 Springs Canyon Campground
highlights.description: Mountain pine forest camping at 8,600ft in Lincoln National Forest — a 35-degree cool-down from the desert below. All forest trails welcome dogs. Hike the aptly-named Dog Canyon Trail in the morning, explore the Osha Trail with Sacramento Mountain rim views, then evening stargazing under dark skies. After 3 days of desert heat management, breathing cool pine-scented mountain air is pure relief.

highlights.name: National Solar Observatory at Sunspot
highlights.description: Free self-guided tours of solar telescopes on the 17-mile Sunspot Scenic Byway south of Cloudcroft

highlights.name: Petroglyph National Monument
highlights.description: 24,000+ ancient petroglyphs on dog-friendly trails — one of the most dog-friendly NPS units

highlights.name: Rio Grande Bosque Trail
highlights.description: 16-mile paved trail along the Rio Grande under shady cottonwoods — perfect evening dog walk

highlights.name: Old Town Albuquerque
highlights.description: Historic 1706 adobe plaza with galleries, chile-heavy restaurants, and Native American art

highlights.name: Santa Fe Plaza
highlights.description: Breakfast stop — Tia Sophia's (invented the breakfast burrito) or The Shed (famous red chile)

highlights.name: Las Vegas, NM
highlights.description: 900+ National Register buildings — genuine Wild West frontier town on the Santa Fe Trail

highlights.name: Raton Pass
highlights.description: 7,834-foot pass through the Sangre de Cristo Mountains on the historic Santa Fe Trail

highlights.name: Garden of the Gods
highlights.description: 300-foot red sandstone formations against Pikes Peak — ALL 21 miles of trails dog-friendly, free entry

routes.name: The Yellowstone Trail
routes.theme: The great northern arc through Nevada's basin-and-range, Idaho's volcanic plains, Yellowstone's geothermal wonders, and Wyoming's Big Horn country — following the path of America's first transcontinental highway

highlights.name: Donner Memorial State Park
highlights.description: Historic pass at 7,056ft. Stop at the visitor center for the harrowing Donner Party story. Short nature trails through pine forest along Donner Lake. Dogs on leash on all trails. Beautiful alpine lake setting with granite peaks.

highlights.name: Truckee, CA (en route)
highlights.description: Charming mountain town 30 min west of Reno. Historic downtown with dog-friendly patios. Excellent coffee at Coffeebar. Quick stretch stop before descending into Nevada.

highlights.name: Virginia Lake Park & Off-Leash Dog Park
highlights.description: Reno city park with a 1-mile path around a scenic lake. Dedicated off-leash dog park with separate areas for small and large dogs, agility equipment, benches, and shade. Perfect evening dog stop after the drive.

highlights.name: Reno Riverwalk District
highlights.description: Revitalized downtown along the Truckee River. Dog-friendly patios at Wild River Grille and The Eddy. Walkable with public art, river views, and evening energy. Great Basin Brewing Co. is Reno's oldest brewery with a dog-friendly patio.

highlights.name: Shoshone Falls
highlights.description: 212-foot waterfall wider than Niagara Falls (900ft across). Peak flow in spring/early June from snowmelt. $5 vehicle entry. Dogs on leash welcome throughout the park -- easy terrain, well-maintained paths, picnic areas, grills, benches, restrooms. Best viewed from the upper overlook platform in late afternoon light.

highlights.name: Perrine Bridge & Snake River Canyon
highlights.description: 486ft above the Snake River. This is where Evel Knievel attempted his rocket-powered jump in 1974. Today it is a legal BASE jumping site -- you will likely see jumpers launching off the bridge. Pedestrian walkway with views of the canyon. Dog-friendly walkway.

highlights.name: Centennial Waterfront Park
highlights.description: Riverside park along the Snake River below the canyon rim. Walking trails, playgrounds, river access. Dogs on leash welcome. Great morning or evening walk with views up at the canyon walls.

highlights.name: Dierkes Lake
highlights.description: Adjacent to Shoshone Falls. Swimming, cliff jumping, hiking trails around a basalt-rimmed desert canyon lake. Dogs allowed on trails but not in the grassy beach area. Beautiful setting.

highlights.name: Craters of the Moon National Monument (optional detour)
highlights.description: Vast otherworldly lava field covering 750,000 acres with cinder cones, lava tubes, and spatter cones. 7-mile loop drive with short walks from pullouts. Dogs NOT allowed on trails (sharp volcanic rock cuts paws) but can ride the loop drive and visit parking areas and the campground. $20 vehicle entry. Worth 1-2 hours for the moonscape scenery.

highlights.name: Idaho Falls River Walk
highlights.description: 5-mile paved path along the Snake River through downtown. Passes the Idaho Falls -- a dramatic man-made waterfall stretching across the full river width. Beautifully landscaped with benches, gardens, and river views. Dogs on leash welcome. Best in late afternoon light.

highlights.name: Freeman Park Off-Leash Dog Area
highlights.description: Fenced off-leash dog park on the south side of Idaho Falls with water stations. Perfect for letting the dog run after the drive and before heading into Yellowstone country where off-leash opportunities are limited.

highlights.name: Tautphaus Park
highlights.description: Large city park with walking paths, playground, picnic areas, and shade trees. Dog-friendly throughout. Good stretch stop with plenty of grass and open space.

highlights.name: Upper Mesa Falls (en route)
highlights.description: 114-foot waterfall on the Henrys Fork river -- Idaho's last major undammed waterfall. Short boardwalk trail to a spectacular viewpoint. $5 vehicle fee. Dogs on leash welcome on the boardwalk and viewing areas. 7 miles off US-20 via Mesa Falls Scenic Byway. Do not miss this stop.

highlights.name: Rendezvous Ski Trails (Summer Hiking)
highlights.description: 30+ km of groomed Nordic ski trails that become hiking/mountain biking trails in summer. Rolling terrain through lodgepole pine and meadows right from town. Dogs on leash welcome. Excellent afternoon hike -- you may see moose, deer, and elk in the meadows.

highlights.name: Grizzly & Wolf Discovery Center
highlights.description: Wildlife park in downtown West Yellowstone with rescued grizzly bears, gray wolves, and raptors. Educational non-profit. Dogs not inside but shaded outdoor waiting area. Take turns visiting while one person walks the dog.

highlights.name: Hebgen Lake
highlights.description: Scenic mountain lake 15 min north of town. Walk the shoreline at sunset. Earthquake Lake nearby (formed by the 1959 Hebgen Lake earthquake). Dogs welcome on the lakeshore. Bring fishing gear for an evening cast.

highlights.name: Old Faithful & Upper Geyser Basin
highlights.description: 45-minute drive from West Yellowstone. World's most famous geyser erupts every 60-110 minutes. Dogs must stay in the car or within 100ft of parking lot -- take turns watching the dog while one person walks the boardwalk. Upper Geyser Basin has the largest concentration of geysers on Earth. Go before 9 AM for fewer crowds and cooler temps for dog in car.

highlights.name: Grand Prismatic Spring (roadside view)
highlights.description: Largest hot spring in the US, third largest in the world. Rainbow colors (orange, yellow, green, blue) are most vivid on warm, calm days. The overlook trail is NOT dog-friendly, but the Midway Geyser Basin parking lot provides a close-up perspective from 100ft of the road with steam and color visible.

highlights.name: Madison River Valley Wildlife Drive
highlights.description: 14-mile drive from the west entrance to Madison Junction along the Madison River through wide meadows. Bison herds grazing, elk foraging, wolves occasionally spotted at dawn and dusk. Dogs can be in the car with windows down. Multiple pullouts for photography. The most accessible Yellowstone wildlife viewing from a vehicle.

highlights.name: Coffin Lake Trails
highlights.description: 11.3-mile trail system in Gallatin National Forest west of town. Dense forest with meadow openings. Less trafficked than Rendezvous trails. Dogs on leash welcome. Moderate terrain. Good afternoon outing while one person goes into the park.

highlights.name: Baker's Hole Campground
highlights.description: Riverside Gallatin National Forest camping on the Madison River, 3 miles north of West Yellowstone — the quintessential Yellowstone base camp. Wake to mist rising off the river with bison grazing in the meadow across the water. Morning drive into the park to see Old Faithful and Grand Prismatic, afternoon dog hike on the Rendezvous Trails through lodgepole forest, evening campfire with elk bugling in the distance. 75F days, 35F nights at 6,600ft — bring warm layers.

highlights.name: Grand Canyon of the Yellowstone (Artist Point)
highlights.description: The Yellowstone River plunges 308 feet over the Lower Falls into a dramatically colorful canyon of yellow, orange, and red rock. Artist Point provides the iconic view. Dogs must stay within 100ft of parking lot -- take turns. One of the most photographed scenes in the US national park system.

highlights.name: Hayden Valley Wildlife Viewing
highlights.description: Wide glacial valley along the Yellowstone River between Canyon Village and Fishing Bridge. Large bison herds, elk, grizzly bears, trumpeter swans. Best at dawn and dusk. Multiple pullouts for viewing from the car. Dogs in the vehicle with windows down.

highlights.name: Wapiti Valley -- Buffalo Bill Scenic Byway
highlights.description: 52 miles from Yellowstone's east entrance to Cody through Shoshone National Forest. Teddy Roosevelt called this the most scenic 52 miles in America. Towering rock formations, North Fork Shoshone River, lodgepole pine forest. Multiple Shoshone NF trailheads where dogs are welcome on leash. Stop at Pahaska Tepee (Buffalo Bill's original hunting lodge) near the east entrance.

highlights.name: Buffalo Bill Center of the West
highlights.description: Five museums in one complex: Buffalo Bill Museum, Whitney Western Art Museum, Plains Indian Museum, Draper Natural History Museum, Cody Firearms Museum. One of the finest museum complexes in the American West. Dogs not inside but shaded courtyard area. $24 adult. Allow 2-3 hours if visiting.

highlights.name: Shell Falls
highlights.description: 120-foot waterfall in Shell Canyon on the Bighorn Scenic Byway. Short wheelchair-accessible walk from parking to overlook platform. Interpretive signs about local geology. Dogs on leash welcome. Free. Canyon walls rise 2,500 feet around you.

highlights.name: Bighorn Scenic Byway (US-14 over Big Horns)
highlights.description: 44-mile scenic drive climbing from 4,200ft to 9,033ft over Granite Pass. Transitions from sagebrush basin through red rock canyons, pine forest, alpine meadows, and back down. One of America's most underrated scenic drives. Multiple pullouts and trailheads with dog-friendly access.

highlights.name: Tongue River Canyon Trail
highlights.description: 4.7-mile moderate trail through a limestone canyon in Bighorn National Forest. Dog-friendly on leash. The canyon walls create a cool microclimate. Stunning rock walls and river scenery. Access from the western slope near Dayton.

highlights.name: Sheridan Historic Main Street
highlights.description: One of the most authentic Western main streets in America. King's Saddlery (since 1946, Western saddles and ropes, free Don King Museum in the back). The Mint Bar (historic watering hole with original neon signs). Dog-friendly sidewalks throughout.

highlights.name: Hole in the Wall Country (near Kaycee, WY)
highlights.description: Butch Cassidy's Wild Bunch hideout. The actual Hole in the Wall is a narrow passage through the Red Wall -- a 35-mile sandstone escarpment. The town of Kaycee has the Hoofprints of the Past Museum. Quick stop off I-25 for outlaw history.

highlights.name: Morad Park Off-Leash Dog Area (Casper)
highlights.description: Beautiful unfenced off-leash dog area right next to the North Platte River in Casper. Benches, picnic table, river access for dog swimming. One of the best off-leash dog stops in Wyoming. THE midday break highlight on the long drive south.

highlights.name: Ayres Natural Bridge Park
highlights.description: One of only three natural bridges in the US with water flowing beneath it. Small county park with picnic areas, hiking paths, and LaPrele Creek. Free admission. Dogs on leash. 45 minutes east of Casper, just off I-25 near Douglas. A hidden gem stretch stop.

highlights.name: Casper, WY (lunch stop)
highlights.description: Wyoming's second-largest city at the North Platte River confluence. National Historic Trails Interpretive Center (Oregon, Mormon, California, Pony Express trails all crossed here). Tate Geological Museum (free, fossils). Lake McKenzie Dog Park has a fenced area with dog drinking fountains, wash hoses, and a swimming area.

routes.name: Death Valley & Beyond
routes.theme: Extreme desert crossing from the lowest point in North America through Navajo country — WARNING: Death Valley reaches 110F+ in June

highlights.name: Trona Pinnacles
highlights.description: 500+ tufa spires rising 140ft from a dry lake bed — filming location for Star Trek V and Westworld, dog-friendly BLM land

highlights.name: Death Valley - Zabriskie Point
highlights.description: Iconic eroded badlands viewpoint — magnificent at sunrise, arrive before the 110F heat

highlights.name: Death Valley - Badwater Basin
highlights.description: Lowest point in North America at 282ft below sea level — surreal white salt flats stretching to the horizon

highlights.name: Wildrose Charcoal Kilns
highlights.description: Ten massive beehive-shaped 1870s kilns at higher elevation — cooler than the valley floor

highlights.name: Vermilion Cliffs
highlights.description: 3,000-foot walls of red and orange sandstone — California condors frequently spotted soaring above

highlights.name: Navajo Bridge
highlights.description: Walk across for views 470 feet down to the Colorado River cutting through Marble Canyon

highlights.name: Horseshoe Bend
highlights.description: Iconic 1,000-foot-deep Colorado River meander — dogs allowed on the 1.5-mile trail, visit at sunrise for cool temps

highlights.name: Lake Powell
highlights.description: Stunning man-made lake with red rock canyons — rent kayaks at Wahweap Marina, dogs welcome on the water

highlights.name: Wahweap Campground
highlights.description: Lakeside camping at Wahweap Campground on the shore of Lake Powell — turquoise water against red sandstone cliffs. Hike Horseshoe Bend with the dog in the morning (dogs welcome on the trail), rent a kayak on the lake in the afternoon, and watch sunset turn the canyon walls crimson from your waterfront campsite. Stargazing over the lake at night.

highlights.name: Glen Canyon Dam
highlights.description: Massive concrete dam creating Lake Powell — visitor center with views of the dam and lake

highlights.name: Four Corners Monument
highlights.description: Only place in the US where four states meet — stand in Colorado, New Mexico, Arizona, and Utah at once

highlights.name: Mesa Verde National Park
highlights.description: 700-year-old Puebloan cliff dwellings — dogs in campgrounds and on paved roads, not trails

highlights.name: Durango Animas River Trail
highlights.description: 7-mile paved trail along the river through town — perfect arrival stroll with the dog

highlights.name: Smelter Mountain Off-Leash
highlights.description: 24-acre off-leash dog area with river access at the base of Smelter Mountain — Sabby's paradise

highlights.name: Carver Brewing Company
highlights.description: Dog-friendly patio brewery in historic downtown — great craft beer and pub food after a rest day

highlights.name: Wolf Creek Pass
highlights.description: 10,856-foot mountain pass with sweeping views — one of Colorado's great mountain crossings

highlights.name: San Luis Valley
highlights.description: Wide-open high desert valley rimmed by the Sangre de Cristo Mountains — Great Sand Dunes visible in distance

highlights.name: Great Sand Dunes (distant view)
highlights.description: North America's tallest sand dunes at 750ft — visible from the highway, worth a future dedicated trip

highlights.name: Fairplay / South Park
highlights.description: The real South Park — high-altitude grassland basin town that inspired the TV show

routes.name: The Pacific Northwest Loop
routes.theme: Cascade volcanoes, Oregon brewery culture, Idaho mountain valleys, and Wyoming's Grand Tetons

highlights.name: Sacramento
highlights.description: Old Sacramento Waterfront — dog-friendly boardwalks along the river, historic buildings, Tower Bridge photo op

highlights.name: Lake Siskiyou
highlights.description: 7-mile shoreline trail reflecting 14,179ft Mt. Shasta in the water — kayaking, swimming, camp resort with dog park

highlights.name: Mount Shasta Headwaters
highlights.description: Crystal-clear natural spring headwaters of the Sacramento River — free, short walk, dog-friendly

highlights.name: Crater Lake National Park
highlights.description: Deepest lake in the US (1,943ft) inside a collapsed volcano — impossibly blue water, dogs on Godfrey Glen Trail

highlights.name: Pilot Butte
highlights.description: 1-mile hike up a cinder cone for 360-degree views of Mt. Hood, Three Sisters, and Mt. Bachelor

highlights.name: Deschutes River Trail
highlights.description: 12-mile trail along the river through Bend — perfect dog walk with craft brewery stops along the way

highlights.name: Tumalo Falls
highlights.description: 97-foot waterfall in Deschutes National Forest, 20 minutes from town — dog-friendly trail, moderate hike

highlights.name: Crux Fermentation Project
highlights.description: Award-winning craft brewery with spacious dog patio and views of the Cascade volcanoes

highlights.name: Tumalo State Park Campground
highlights.description: Riverside camping at Tumalo State Park on the Deschutes River — Bend is America's outdoor brewery capital with 30+ dog-friendly brewery patios. Morning hike to 97-foot Tumalo Falls with the dog, afternoon brewery crawl (Crux, GoodLife, Worthy Brewing — all have spacious dog patios), river swimming at your campsite. 75F days, 40F nights — perfect dog weather after desert routes.

highlights.name: Worthy Brewing Observatory
highlights.description: Brewery with an actual astronomical observatory — craft beer, dog patio, stargazing through a real telescope

highlights.name: Shevlin Park
highlights.description: 990-acre park with off-leash areas and miles of forested trails along Tumalo Creek

highlights.name: Painted Hills
highlights.description: One of Oregon's Seven Wonders — surreal banded volcanic ash hills in red, gold, black, dogs on ALL trails

highlights.name: John Day Fossil Beds - Sheep Rock Unit
highlights.description: 40-million-year-old fossil beds in dramatic canyon scenery — Thomas Condon Paleontology Center, dogs on roads and parking areas

highlights.name: Kam Wah Chung State Heritage Site
highlights.description: Remarkably preserved 1860s Chinese medicine and general store — unique piece of Eastern Oregon frontier history

highlights.name: Blue Mountain Scenic Byway
highlights.description: Winding mountain highway through Malheur National Forest — old-growth ponderosa pines, elk, and wildflower meadows

highlights.name: Boise River Greenbelt
highlights.description: 25-mile paved pathway along the Boise River — trees, parks, swimming access, fully dog-friendly

highlights.name: Freak Alley Gallery
highlights.description: Largest outdoor mural gallery in the Pacific Northwest — free, walkable with dog

highlights.name: Hemingway Memorial
highlights.description: Stone memorial along Trail Creek — Hemingway wrote 'For Whom the Bell Tolls' here and is buried in Ketchum

highlights.name: Draper Preserve
highlights.description: Wooded path along Big Wood River — dog swimming paradise with stick-fetching in the river, off-leash under voice control

highlights.name: Galena Summit Overlook
highlights.description: 8,701ft overlook of the Sawtooth Mountains and Salmon River headwaters — one of Idaho's most iconic vistas

highlights.name: Sawtooth Brewery
highlights.description: Dog-friendly patio brewery with house-brewed beers — relaxed mountain town dining

highlights.name: Craters of the Moon
highlights.description: Vast lava field with cinder cones and lava tubes — 7-mile loop drive, looks like the moon's surface

highlights.name: Grand Teton NP - Mormon Row
highlights.description: Most photographed barn in America with the Teton Range rising 7,000ft behind — dogs at roadside pullouts

highlights.name: Cache Creek Trail System
highlights.description: Dog-friendly trail system in Bridger-Teton NF just east of Jackson — where locals hike with dogs

highlights.name: Togwotee Pass
highlights.description: 9,658-foot pass through alpine meadows with views of the Absaroka and Wind River Ranges behind you

highlights.name: Dubois, WY
highlights.description: Small Western town (pop. 971) with the National Bighorn Sheep Interpretive Center — good breakfast stop

highlights.name: Elk Mountain & Medicine Bow
highlights.description: Historic I-80 landmarks — Elk Mountain rises 11,156ft above the high plains, visible for 50 miles. The old Union Pacific railroad town of Medicine Bow inspired Owen Wister's 'The Virginian', the first Western novel

highlights.name: Cheyenne
highlights.description: Wyoming's state capital — historic downtown, Union Pacific Depot, Big Boy steam locomotive display

routes.name: Eastern Sierra Scenic
routes.theme: Mt. Whitney, ancient bristlecone pines, and California's alpine lake country

highlights.name: Pinnacles National Park (West Entrance)
highlights.description: Volcanic spires and talus caves — dogs on Bench Trail only but the scenic approach through the Salinas Valley is beautiful

highlights.name: Sequoia Gateway
highlights.description: Visalia is the gateway to Sequoia National Park — explore charming downtown with dog-friendly patios and breweries

highlights.name: Alabama Hills
highlights.description: Iconic rounded boulders against Mt. Whitney — BLM land where Iron Man, Gladiator, Django were filmed, dogs welcome everywhere

highlights.name: Mobius Arch Trail
highlights.description: Short hike to a natural granite arch framing 14,505ft Mt. Whitney — one of California's most photographed spots

highlights.name: Movie Road
highlights.description: Self-guided driving tour through rock formations used in hundreds of movies — sunset here is extraordinary

highlights.name: Whitney Portal Road
highlights.description: 13-mile climb toward the Mt. Whitney trailhead at 8,374ft for close-up Sierra Nevada views

highlights.name: Manzanar National Historic Site
highlights.description: Preserved WWII Japanese American internment camp with powerful museum — dogs in outdoor areas on leash

highlights.name: Erick Schat's Bakkery Bishop
highlights.description: Legendary bakery since 1938 — famous sheepherder bread and pastries, a Highway 395 institution

highlights.name: Ancient Bristlecone Pine Forest
highlights.description: Home to 4,500-year-old trees — oldest living organisms on Earth, dogs on leash in Inyo National Forest

highlights.name: Wild Willy's Hot Spring
highlights.description: Free natural hot springs with stunning Sierra Nevada views on both sides — dogs around springs (not in water)

highlights.name: Convict Lake
highlights.description: Deep cobalt-blue lake enclosed by colorful 12,000ft cliffs — 3-mile dog-friendly loop trail

highlights.name: Mammoth Mountain Scenic Gondola
highlights.description: Ride to 11,053ft summit for panoramic Sierra views — leashed dogs welcome on the gondola in summer

highlights.name: Horseshoe Lake Dog Beach
highlights.description: Unofficial dog swimming spot where locals bring their dogs — flat loop trail through lodgepole pines

highlights.name: June Lake Loop
highlights.description: 16-mile scenic loop with four alpine lakes — stunning half-day drive with swimming and fishing stops

highlights.name: Twin Lakes Campground
highlights.description: Mountain lake camping at Twin Lakes Campground (8,600ft) in the Mammoth Lakes Basin. Mammoth is arguably the most dog-friendly mountain town in California — dogs ride the scenic gondola to 11,053ft, swim at Horseshoe Lake's unofficial dog beach, and roam off-leash in wilderness areas. 65F days, cool nights, six alpine lakes within walking distance. After the high desert, this is an alpine paradise.

highlights.name: Lakes Basin Drive
highlights.description: Drive past six pristine alpine lakes — Twin Lakes, Lake Mary, Lake Mamie, Lake George, Horseshoe Lake

highlights.name: Mono Lake South Tufa
highlights.description: Otherworldly calcium-carbonate towers rising from an ancient alkaline lake — best at sunset, dog-friendly

highlights.name: Mizpah Hotel
highlights.description: Beautifully restored 1907 hotel — voted #1 Haunted Hotel in America, pet-friendly rooms

highlights.name: Tonopah Stargazing
highlights.description: #1 stargazing destination in America — designated dark sky community with extraordinary night skies

highlights.name: Nevada Northern Railway
highlights.description: Ride a historic steam locomotive through the Nevada desert — one of America's best-preserved railroad museums

highlights.name: Cave Lake State Park
highlights.description: 32-acre reservoir with trout fishing, hiking trails, and shoreline walks — dogs welcome on leash

highlights.name: Ward Charcoal Ovens
highlights.description: Six massive beehive-shaped stone charcoal ovens from 1876 — hiking trails through pinyon-juniper forest

highlights.name: Great Basin NP (distant view)
highlights.description: 70 miles from Ely — optional detour for Wheeler Peak Scenic Drive to 10,000ft and Lehman Caves

highlights.name: San Rafael Swell
highlights.description: Dramatic eroded red rock formations, canyons, and mesas visible from I-70 through central Utah

highlights.name: Glenwood Canyon
highlights.description: 12.5 miles of I-70 carved through 2,000-foot canyon walls along the Colorado River

highlights.name: Glenwood Springs
highlights.description: Walk the dog on the Glenwood Canyon Recreation Trail (paved, 16mi) — grab lunch at Bluebird Cafe

highlights.name: Eisenhower Tunnel
highlights.description: Highest point on the US Interstate system at 11,158ft — crossing the Continental Divide

routes.name: Coastal & I-40 Corridor
routes.theme: California coast start, Petrified Forest dog paradise, Coconino pine forest camping, and Garden of the Gods

highlights.name: Pinnacles National Park
highlights.description: Volcanic spires and talus caves — dogs restricted to Bench Trail only but the drive-through is spectacular

highlights.name: San Luis Obispo Downtown
highlights.description: Charming college town with Thursday farmers market, creek walk, and mission — many dog-friendly patios

highlights.name: Morro Rock
highlights.description: Iconic 576-foot volcanic plug — dog-friendly Morro Strand State Beach for an evening walk

highlights.name: Route 66 Mother Road Museum
highlights.description: Housed in the restored 1911 Casa del Desierto Harvey House — free museum of Route 66 history

highlights.name: Calico Ghost Town
highlights.description: 1880s silver mining ghost town with original buildings — walking tours, dog-friendly on leash

highlights.name: Snow Cap Drive-In Seligman
highlights.description: Quirky Route 66 roadside diner with fake mustard bottles and classic cars — photo-op institution

highlights.name: Route 66 Seligman
highlights.description: The birthplace of the Route 66 preservation movement — inspired the Cars movie town of Radiator Springs

highlights.name: Flagstaff Historic Downtown
highlights.description: World's first International Dark Sky City — craft breweries, Northern Arizona University, ponderosa pine forest

highlights.name: Sunset Crater Lava Flow Trail
highlights.description: Surreal black volcanic cinder landscape — 1,000-year-old lava fields, dog-friendly on leash

highlights.name: Bonito Campground
highlights.description: Ponderosa pine forest camping at Bonito Campground (6,900ft) in Coconino National Forest — next to Sunset Crater volcano. Wake to elk wandering through camp, hike the surreal black lava Sunset Crater trail with the dog, explore Walnut Canyon cliff dwellings (Rim Trail is dog-friendly), and stargaze under the world's first Dark Sky City. The ponderosa bark smells like butterscotch in the warm sun. 78F days, 40F nights — a forest oasis after desert driving.

highlights.name: Walnut Canyon National Monument
highlights.description: 800-year-old cliff dwellings — Rim Trail (0.9mi) is dog-friendly with canyon views

highlights.name: Fatman's Loop Trail
highlights.description: Dog-friendly mountain trail with panoramic views of Flagstaff and the San Francisco Peaks

highlights.name: Lake Mary
highlights.description: Two alpine lakes 15 minutes from Flagstaff — dog swimming, fishing, forest picnicking at 7,000ft

highlights.name: Meteor Crater
highlights.description: 550-foot-deep, mile-wide impact crater — Apollo astronauts trained here. Pet Ramada and off-leash dog runs

highlights.name: Petrified Forest National Park
highlights.description: The most dog-friendly NPS site — dogs on ALL trails. Ancient petrified wood across Painted Desert badlands. BARK Ranger program

highlights.name: Standin' on the Corner Park Winslow
highlights.description: Eagles 'Take It Easy' tribute with Glenn Frey statue and red flatbed Ford truck

highlights.name: Route 66 Neon Arch Grants
highlights.description: Iconic neon arch spanning Route 66 — classic Americana photo stop in this uranium mining town

highlights.name: Canyon Road Santa Fe
highlights.description: Half-mile of 80+ art galleries, many welcoming dogs — the art capital of the American Southwest

highlights.name: Frank Ortiz Dog Park
highlights.description: 138 acres of off-leash paradise with mountain views — one of the best dog parks in the Southwest

highlights.name: Raton Pass
highlights.description: 7,834-foot Santa Fe Trail pass through the Sangre de Cristo Mountains into Colorado

highlights.name: Spanish Peaks
highlights.description: Sacred twin volcanic peaks rising above the plains — dramatic views from I-25

highlights.name: Garden of the Gods
highlights.description: 300-foot red sandstone formations against Pikes Peak — ALL 21 miles of trails dog-friendly, free entry

highlights.name: Cherry Creek State Park
highlights.description: 107-acre off-leash dog area — perfect victory lap for Sabby after 8 days on the road

highlights.name: Palmer Park
highlights.description: Dog-friendly mesa-top park in Colorado Springs with red rock formations and mountain views

routes.name: Montana Big Sky
routes.theme: Lewis & Clark history, Montana big sky country, mountain town culture, Bighorn Mountains, and Wyoming ranch country

highlights.name: Bidwell Park -- Upper Park
highlights.description: 3,670-acre municipal park, 3rd largest in the US. Upper Park has swimming holes in Big Chico Creek, basalt formations, and oak woodlands. The swimming scene from the 1938 Robin Hood was filmed at Bear Hole. Dogs allowed on leash throughout; off-leash in designated areas.

highlights.name: Bidwell Park -- Lower Park & One-Mile Recreation Area
highlights.description: Paved paths under massive sycamores and oaks along Big Chico Creek. Sycamore Pool for swimming. Flat, easy walking with the dog. One of the most beautiful urban creekside parks in California.

highlights.name: Sierra Nevada Brewing Company
highlights.description: Iconic craft brewery campus with taproom, restaurant, extensive gardens, and dog-friendly patio. One of the most important craft breweries in American history. Self-guided garden tour.

highlights.name: Downtown Chico
highlights.description: Walkable downtown with galleries, bookshops, cafes, and restaurants. Thursday Night Market in summer. Dog-friendly patios at Madison Bear Garden, The Banshee, and The Handlebar.

highlights.name: Feather River Canyon (en route)
highlights.description: CA-70 follows the Feather River through a deep canyon for 80+ miles. Railroad bridges, tunnels visible across the gorge, river swimming holes. One of NorCal's most underrated scenic drives. Multiple pullouts for dog walks.

highlights.name: Winnemucca Sand Dunes
highlights.description: BLM-managed sand dunes just north of town. Open for walking, OHV riding, photography. Dogs welcome off-leash on BLM land. Unexpected desert landscape.

highlights.name: Martin Hotel -- Basque Dining
highlights.description: Historic 1898 Basque boardinghouse serving family-style Basque dinners. Lamb chops, Picon punch, communal tables. An authentic slice of Northern Nevada's Basque heritage.

highlights.name: Humboldt River Walk
highlights.description: Paved riverwalk along the Humboldt River through town. Evening dog walk with cottonwood shade. Connected to Winnemucca city parks.

highlights.name: Shoshone Falls (en route, Twin Falls)
highlights.description: 212-foot waterfall on the Snake River -- taller than Niagara Falls. Called the 'Niagara of the West.' $5 vehicle fee. Dogs welcome on leash throughout the park. Canyon Rim Trail (9 miles) connects to the falls. Best flow in May-June from snowmelt.

highlights.name: Boise River Greenbelt
highlights.description: 25-mile paved pathway along the Boise River through the heart of the city. Mature cottonwoods, parks, river swimming access. Fully dog-friendly. Best urban river trail in the Mountain West.

highlights.name: Camel's Back Park & Ridge to Rivers Trails
highlights.description: 11-acre park connecting to 190+ miles of foothill trails. Off-leash dog area. Views of the city and the Boise Front mountain range. Perfect sunset hike spot.

highlights.name: Freak Alley Gallery
highlights.description: Largest outdoor mural gallery in the Pacific Northwest. Downtown alleyway covered in murals by local artists. Free, walkable with dog. Between 8th and 9th Streets.

highlights.name: Traveler's Rest State Park
highlights.description: The ONLY Lewis & Clark campsite with confirmed archaeological evidence. The expedition camped here Sept 9-11, 1805 before crossing the Bitterroots, and again June 30 - July 3, 1806 on the return trip. Trails along Lolo Creek, 115 bird species recorded. Dogs welcome on leash throughout. $8 out-of-state.

highlights.name: Rattlesnake National Recreation Area
highlights.description: 61,000-acre wilderness area just north of Missoula. Miles of dog-friendly trails through pristine forest, crystal-clear streams, and mountain meadows. Rattlesnake Creek Trail is an easy 4-mile out-and-back. Dogs off-leash under voice control in wilderness areas.

highlights.name: Mount Sentinel 'M' Trail
highlights.description: Iconic switchback trail climbing 2,000 feet above Missoula to the concrete 'M' overlooking the University of Montana. Panoramic views of the entire Missoula Valley and five surrounding mountain ranges. Dogs welcome on leash.

highlights.name: Carousel for Missoula & Caras Park
highlights.description: Hand-carved carousel downtown on the Clark Fork River. Caras Park hosts summer events along the riverfront. Leashed dogs welcome in the park area. Walk across the Higgins Avenue Bridge for river views and Clark Fork Trail access.

highlights.name: Gates of the Mountains Viewpoint (en route, near Helena)
highlights.description: Lewis named this dramatic Missouri River canyon where 1,200-foot limestone cliffs appear to open like a gate as you approach. Boat tours available (no dogs on boats) but the viewpoint and surrounding Helena National Forest trails are dog-friendly. 20 miles north of Helena off I-15.

highlights.name: Hyalite Canyon Recreation Area
highlights.description: 15 miles south of Bozeman. 30,000-acre recreation area in Gallatin National Forest with multiple waterfalls (Palisade Falls, Grotto Falls), Hyalite Reservoir, and trails to alpine lakes. Dogs welcome throughout on leash; off-leash in some backcountry areas. Three campgrounds open May 15-Sept 15.

highlights.name: Peets Hill / Burke Park
highlights.description: 2-mile loop trail in the heart of Bozeman with 360-degree views of the Bridger Range, Gallatin Range, and Spanish Peaks. Popular sunset spot. Off-leash friendly. The most iconic Bozeman walk.

highlights.name: Downtown Bozeman Main Street
highlights.description: Historic Main Street lined with independent bookshops (Country Bookshelf), galleries, outdoor gear shops (Schnee's Boots), and farm-to-table restaurants. Dog-friendly patios everywhere. The vibe of a Western town that grew up without losing its character.

highlights.name: Palisade Falls
highlights.description: 80-foot waterfall in Hyalite Canyon. Easy 1.2-mile round trip on a paved/gravel path through dense forest. ADA accessible. One of the most accessible waterfalls in Montana. Dogs on leash.

highlights.name: Grotto Falls
highlights.description: Beautiful waterfall at the end of a 2.5-mile forested trail in Hyalite Canyon. You can walk behind the falls. Less crowded than Palisade. Dogs on leash.

highlights.name: Hyalite Reservoir
highlights.description: Alpine reservoir camping at Hyalite Canyon (6,800ft) in Gallatin National Forest — Montana's best-kept secret. Wake to mist rising off the reservoir with the Hyalite Peaks reflected in the water. Morning hike to Palisade Falls (80ft, paved trail) or Grotto Falls (walk behind the waterfall), afternoon dog swimming from the rocky shoreline, evening campfire under the biggest sky you've ever seen. Cutthroat trout fishing, kayak rentals nearby. 75F days, 40F nights — true Big Sky Country camping.

highlights.name: Beehive Basin Trail (Big Sky detour)
highlights.description: 3-mile one-way hike to an alpine basin at 9,200ft with wildflower meadows and panoramic views of Lone Mountain and the Spanish Peaks. One of the best dog-friendly hikes in Montana. 50 miles south of Bozeman in Big Sky.

highlights.name: Little Bighorn Battlefield National Monument (en route)
highlights.description: Site of the 1876 Battle of the Little Bighorn where Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho warriors defeated Custer's 7th Cavalry. Self-guided tour of battlefield, monuments, and national cemetery. Dogs allowed on leash in parking areas and along paved paths but NOT on the battlefield trails. One of the most significant sites in American Western history.

highlights.name: Bighorn Scenic Byway (US-14)
highlights.description: 57-mile scenic byway climbing from Sheridan/Dayton through Bighorn National Forest. Prairie to alpine in 30 miles. Shell Falls (120ft waterfall with boardwalk), Burgess Junction, and Granite Pass (9,033ft). Dogs welcome on all National Forest trails on leash. 1,200 miles of trails in Bighorn NF.

highlights.name: Historic Downtown Sheridan & The Mint Bar
highlights.description: Victorian-era Main Street with Western shops, galleries, and the legendary Mint Bar -- walls covered in taxidermy, saddles for barstools, Rainier neon signs. King's Saddlery next door has a museum of Western memorabilia. Authentic Western small-town America.

highlights.name: Tongue River Trail & Kendrick Park
highlights.description: Paved trail system along the Tongue River through Sheridan. Kendrick Park has a dog-friendly area, creek access, and mature cottonwoods. Perfect morning or evening dog walk before or after the drive.

highlights.name: Buffalo, WY -- Clear Creek Trail
highlights.description: Charming Western town with 20+ miles of Clear Creek Trail system running through it. Dog-friendly trails along the creek with mountain views. Good coffee stop at The Busy Bee or Sagewood Cafe. Jim Gatchell Memorial Museum tells the story of the Johnson County War.

highlights.name: National Historic Trails Interpretive Center (Casper)
highlights.description: 11,000-sq-ft museum telling the story of the Oregon, Mormon, California, and Pony Express trails -- all converged at Casper's river crossing. Interactive exhibits, film presentations. Dogs not allowed inside; partner takes turns. Free admission.

highlights.name: Ayres Natural Bridge Park
highlights.description: One of only three natural bridges in the US with water flowing beneath. Small county park with picnic areas, hiking paths, and LaPrele Creek. Free admission. Dogs on leash. 45 minutes east of Casper, just off I-25.

highlights.name: Cheyenne, WY -- State Capitol
highlights.description: Wyoming's capital city. Walk around the gold-domed State Capitol (exterior is dog-friendly). Cheyenne Depot Museum in a beautiful railroad station. Quick stretch stop before the final 2-hour push to Parker.
